_meta.purpose: Operational workflows, universal rules, detection heuristics, cost models, and edge cases for all non-BCBS payers. Supplements individual payer_*.json files.
_meta.notes: Individual payer JSONs have auth/coverage/referral data. This file adds: universal cross-payer rules, HST code routing, OON list, operational workflows, cost models, detection heuristics, escalation paths, and the portal directory.

call_when_in_doubt.description: If any payer's rules are unclear, auth requirement is uncertain, or the plan doesn't match known patterns — call the payer.

medicare_supplement.description: True Medicare Supplement plans (Plan A through Plan N) require no authorization after Medicare pays. Applies regardless of carrier (BCBS, Aetna, UHC, Cigna, etc.).

referral_vs_authorization.description: Some payers separate referral (PCP approval to see specialist) from authorization (payer approval for specific test). A plan may require one, both, or neither.
referral_vs_authorization.action: Check benefits to determine which applies

universal_medicaid.description: All Medicaid plans: 100% covered for approved charges, no patient cost.

chart_notes.description: If chart notes are missing, request them upfront. Some payers allow submission to proceed while waiting.

hst_code_routing._description: Master HST code routing table. Use primary insurance's code. ~1% exception may use secondary's code.

special_rules.nevada_medicaid_ffs: Never allows HST as primary. As secondary, follows primary's code.
special_rules.ebms: TPA — depends on parent network. Always call to verify.
special_rules.umr: 95806 (Sierra/standard) or G0399 (UHC plans). See UMR section.

commercial.referral_npi_rule: PCP NPI + Facility NPI ONLY. Do NOT use specialist/reading physician NPI.

workflow: Check benefits via Availity → determines if PCP referral needed
If PCP referral required: submit ALL codes through Availity referral tool using PCP NPI + Facility NPI ONLY
If in-lab auth required: submit through Availity
Notes: Not required. Only submit if specifically requested.

detection_heuristics.supplement: ID format differs from standard OR card says 'Medicare Supplement'
detection_heuristics.tpa_managed: Portal indicates during verification → call provided number
detection_heuristics.oon_commercial: One Commercial plan (possibly Local Access Plus) self-identifies during benefits verification

workflow: Check ID format/card: Supplement ID or card says 'Medicare Supplement'? → myproviderhq.com, no auth
Verify benefits via CignaforHCP.com → confirms network status, catches OON plan
If TPA-managed → call number provided by portal
Standard in-network Commercial: HST no auth, In-lab submit via Evicore, attach notes if requested

triple_portal_workflow: Check BOTH uhcprovider.com AND Curo for referral requirements and PCP info

surest.description: Location-based variable copays by ZIP code
surest.action: Must call or message to get accurate copay info
surest.warning: NEVER quote copay from portal — always call/message

detection_heuristics.direct_vs_optum: Determined by contract name, card info, or during verification. No ID difference between paths.

optum.note: Use Optum criteria, NOT Humana criteria

detection_heuristics.ipa_identification: Availity shows IPA during benefits check. Card sometimes shows IPA.
detection_heuristics.ipa_verification: If Availity shows an IPA, may need to call to verify (some 'IPAs' are just clinics/facilities).
detection_heuristics.no_ipa_rule: No IPA listed on Availity = no IPA

operational.notes_action: Can submit without notes, proceed while waiting
operational.ownership: Both managed under UHC umbrella (separate insurance, UHC-owned)

workflow: If chart notes missing → request them (universal rule)
Submit ALL codes through provider.healthplanofnevada.com
Don't call to verify — just submit
Can proceed with submission even if notes not yet received
HST code: 95806 unless primary insurance uses G0399

nevada_medicaid_ffs.rx_rule: If Medicaid is primary AND not a replacement → need new Rx if provider ordered HST only
workflow: Medicaid primary? → HST NOT allowed, must be PSG
If Rx says HST only → request new Rx for PSG
Obtain sleep questionnaire from patient
Submit PSG auth via medicaid.nv.gov portal — include sleep questionnaire
Be thorough — they are extremely particular
If Medicaid is SECONDARY → HST allowed, follows primary's code

dod_10_digit.description: DOD number — cannot verify, need SSN or DBN

secondary_rule.description: Tricare is always secondary if patient has other insurance (except Medicaid)

tricare_for_life.verification: Must CALL to verify eligibility (portal won't work)
tricare_for_life.if_eligible: 100% covered after Medicare, no auth any codes

workflow: Check ID format: 10-digit DOD? Cannot verify — need SSN or DBN
Verify eligibility via Availity → note plan name
Use Availity's Tricare code lookup tool: enter plan name + code
Tool indicates if auth/referral required
If required: Tricare payer space in Availity → redirects to Tricare's system (painful)
Receive referral from VA (this IS the authorization)
Must schedule patient within 1 WEEK
If not scheduled in time → VA cancels the referral/auth
100% covered — no patient cost

workers_comp_dol.priority_rule: Do NOT bother with commercial — use DOL only
workflow: Identify DOL coverage (notes clues or patient mention)
Verify ID via owcpconnect.dol.gov portal
If covered under DOL program: No auth required (any codes), 100% covered

medicaid.titration_warning: In-lab titrations almost never approved — still submit and try

medicaid.inlab_note: Submit via Availity for confirmation even though no auth required
medicaid.guidelines_warning: Guidelines change semi-frequently — always submit through Availity to get confirmation

marketplace.note: Rare plan — same workflow as Medicaid, but referral requirement uncertain

verification_workflow: Use procedure code lookup tool to confirm no auth: caresource.com/.../prior-authorization/medicaid/
caresource_medicaid.new_plan_warning: Brand new plan — verify referring physician network status

clark_county_self_funded.portal_bug: Portal quotes benefits incorrectly — always 80/20, never 90/10

mgm_direct.cost: 100/0 — no out of pocket cost to patient

uhc_plans.verification: Verify via UMR portal, call if unable
uhc_plans.note: Only UMR variant that uses G0399 and UHC allowables

coordination_of_benefits.medicaid_exception: Primary to Medicaid (if Medicaid present, ChampVA goes first)

champva.verification: EHR (building) or call — phone wait times exceed 4 hours on average (worst of any payer)

ebms.description: Third party administrator — rules depend on parent network
ebms.verification: Call only — need card for phone number
workflow: Always need the card to call for benefits and authorization requirements

warnings: Subsidiary of UMR — sometimes mislabeled. Uses UHC network and allowables.

lv_firefighters.critical_rule: Even though no auth required, must call to confirm each time
workflow: Call to confirm auth requirements every time
Benefits vary by patient — check each time

multiplan_phcs_first_health.card_warning: Cards are always random/obscure — almost always need the physical card to get correct contact number
workflow: Call to verify — varies by plan
Contact the IBA, not the network directly, for auth/benefits
